title_guess: France & Switzerland: Vitry-le-François, Gray, St Point Lake, Gadmen

pages.text:
We had a good supper at the Plough in our usual Kent campsite

Friday 17th June at 7.30. we left for France. Lots of lorries in layby, Bad queueing system.
We motored south east + spent the night at Camping à la Nature [dt?] A Ventra walk before bed.
near Vitry la France, Luce Mont + Villotte.

Saturday
A leisurely departure. It might be 35 or 7°. England was v. hot just as we left.
We drove 4 hours to Gray + had lunch at the Brasserie overlooking the weir. A boat went down the lock. On for hours. I got fed up. We went to a U supermarket, then on to St. Point Lake. First campsite is full. Lots of motor bikes!
We found a campsite higher in the hills. Miroir at Los Hopitsaux Neufs. 5 villages make up a ski resort lovely small valley. Skiers/mountain bikes. We had a drink in the bar (Wi-Fi) for waiting for Charles to ring, but he didn't.

Sunday We started putting up the awning. 6[?] I broke the support at my end, then Simon broke it at his end. It was v. hot. The awning wouldn't stay up. We recovered by going on a 5m bike ride. Down the valley + up a hill side. The ebikers are wonderful. We then recharged them from solar power. Nice gazpacho + cheese + pate from Supermarket. A nice town. Will the skiing be hit by global warming?

Monday
We left at 8.50. Into Switzerland. Beautiful, neat, clean, lots of road works. Past Bern. Groenwald off to right. Past Interlaken
We reached Gadmen village + the campsite + had lunch. We had [sticker obscures text] bike r[ide] a nap + then set off on a wonderful [illegible] bike ride
We tried all sorts of paths + finally found a concrete one that went up + up. (The rubble ones perhaps are for mtn bikers.) We went up 1100 ft in 3 miles
(another 150ft to top of pass)
V. steep drops. We were above the snowline.
I slipped off the path from not concentrating, but fortunately on the mtn side. Cows, yellow fox-gloves. For 30 min we came downhill with both brakes on squeaking all the way down.

The campsite is excentric. The owner (Dutch?) wanders round in bare feet. Mexican woodworker

does the cooking. Veg garden. Compost heap. Real towel in bathroom. Homemade benches + slide. V nice but oh dear! we were greeted by 2 horse-flies. Masses of flies. V. natural + healthy but fly-ridden.
    Charles has suggested going over the Susten Pass.
    Supper at the campsite restaurant - pizza + chocolate cake. V. full. We are going to use the fly screens tonight.
__Tuesday__ I hand washed some clothes. Simon cleaned the windscreen.
  We set off in the direction we had been cycling. Glaciers. Through the Susten pass. V [?beautiful] but dangerous bends, some straight after tunnels. Mad motorcyclists. After the pass, (a short tunnel) we zigzagged down, going through Dorfi ([?Ibdis] village?) + south through the St. Gotthard Tunnel. 17R long.
[?Later]    On along complicated fast roads, past Lake Lugano + near Como. We went to the Fiamma huge shop that made the awning that broke. They don't have the parts that broke. (probably sold out to other people with busted awnings). 12 other vans in carpark

probably all after bloody broken awning parts. Simon thinks he's bought enough bits to mend it.
  V hot + sweaty. On the to Pavia. Yoga, shower, supper, walk.
  We passed poplar plantations and [green] rice paddy fields. Poplars put up with water, so they are good for making pallettes.
  Walter + Austin have both got into schools for the autumn. It will be the first time that all 5 children will be in school. Olive is 7. She will be 8 in November.
__Wednesday June 22nd.__
  Rain, but still warm. In the morning we swam. [The] I used [used] the washing machine. We cycled into Pavia + went to St. Michael Major church then the cathedral - dome work[ed] in by [Leonardo + Bramante?] - impressive marble. We went on to the big square. While we were in the cathedral Charles telephoned. We saw huge square tower (why built?) in the university quarter + finished our visit at St. Tadeo.

captions: Crucifixe of St. Teodote (?) Abbess Raingarda at its feet. Linked by legend to Abgar, King of Edessa, at time of Christ's death. Thin silver gilded foil.
Archangel Michael (on facade) who crushes the devil-dragon
[?Nativity] Annunciation - but who is the child?
pages.text:

places: Chiesa di San Pietro in Ciel d'Oro
pages.text:
The wind was getting stronger. Simon had mended the awning in the morning & he didn't want it to be blown down while we were away. So we raced home on our bikes. Alas, the [?rain] fell 10 minutes before we got back. So we spent the night festooned in damp clothes. It rained off + on but it was great having the awning.
__Thursday__
We set off at 9.15. back to Pavia. We leave the battery packs at the van, so some of the cycling is a bit of a challenge, but mostly flat. We go back to St. Tades to look at more of the paintings. It's a friendly church. They invite us up to look at the paintings & switch the light on for us. In one [?painting] of the town & towers, there are paddle wheels at the back of the boats. But how do they turn the wheels? Lanfranc (d 1098) Archb[ishop] of Canterbury, was born in Pavia. On we went to Chiesa di San Pietro in Ciel d'Oro where Tomb of St Augustine is. V. ornate. Then we cycled on to the Castle built by

[Photo of mosaic scene]
Death of the Righteous Man

[Printed text block — The floor mosaic]
The restoration of 1863 revealed in the presbytery, a large portion of the Romanesque mosaic floor (Fig. 14) from the first half of the twelfth century. In the middle of a sequence of arcs, the figure of the Year (King) on the throne is flanked on the left by the months of February (churning), March (blowing the horns) and April (holding flowers) and on the right, by May [illegible] (with the sickle), June and July (reap grain). Below is a portion of a labyrinth organized in geometric concentric arcs.

[Photo of mosaic floor with labyrinth and calendar scenes]
St Michael
Major church

[Vertical text in right margin:] Wooden model of Pavia Cathedral

the Visconti Family. There is a large archeological museum + art gallery. Romanesque sculpture. V. stuffy rooms. A huge enclosed area. [?14]. We cycled back + most of the washing was dry.
We swam.

captions: Wooden model of Pavia Cathedral, kept in the Castle. It was made because it was more accurate for making the building than drawings would be. It's huge. It might just fit in our kitchen.
pages.text:
[Vertical text on left side:] Wooden model of Pavia Cathedral, kept in the Castle. It was made because it was more accurate for making the building than drawings would be. It's huge. It might just fit in our kitchen.

[Large photo of wooden model of Pavia Cathedral]

__Friday__ We packed up + 6 st. In the night the fridge kept going on + off so we switched it off. We think it's because there were so many trees, shade, in the campsite. The fridge uses 40w.

We drove to Certosa + the fridge is working again. Certosa is a monastery set up in [?C4] by Visconti family. It still has 6 monks. Beautiful carvings, inlays, frescoes.

[right margin annotations alongside aerial photo:]
25 little houses for ~~most~~ monks.
food put through hatch in wall
downstairs Sitting room chapel garden
up stairs bedroom loggia

Cisterian order founded by St. Bruno (10
work, prayer, meditation, silence, study

[below top photo, left side:]
Small cloister

captions: 5ft high? made of ivory + hippo teeth
pages.text: 5ft high? made of ivory + hippo teeth

We shopped. Well Simon shopped.
We camped after a long, horrible, 4 hour journey (flat crowded motorways) at 'Pian' del Voglio, a lovely rural campsite downhill from the village. This time the campsite owner yet again had a foreign wife? Philippino? Super spot. At Relax campsite (vg)

blue ceiling of church

Saturday June 25th.
We walked up a zig zag path through fruit trees &
crawled out of a hole in the hedge into the road
circling the village. The church is beautiful & plain.
I lit a candle for Joan Grimley. Amazing views of
fields all round. A super [illegible] walk in an old
village with nice modern bits. Friendly people.
An old fort belonging to Medici's next to the church.
It would be good to come back. We bought bread.
Alex said that not only the Medici are

from round here, but also the
della Casas.

Camping Relax
Via del Lavoro, 10 - 40048 Pian del Voglio (BO)
tel/fax +39 0534 98 228 - email: campingrelax@alice.it
www.sophieallport.com
